user.title: Bravia Theatre setup
user.description: Enter the IP address of your Bravia Theatre device.

transport.title: Connection method
transport.description:
Choose how Home Assistant controls your Bravia Theatre.

**gRPC — BRAVIA Connect (recommended)**
Same control plane as the Sony Home Entertainment Connect app: live push updates, now-playing metadata, play/pause/next on Spotify and AirPlay, sound field mode, DSEE Ultimate, 360SSM height, center speaker mode, DTS Dialog Control, and subwoofer level. Requires a one-time Sony account sign-in. **Beta Feature:** still evolving — expect parity gaps vs TCP and occasional changes across releases or firmware.

**TCP — legacy IP control**
Original reverse-engineered protocol (port 33336). No Sony sign-in, but fewer features: no streaming transport controls, no now-playing metadata, and no BRAVIA Connect-only sound settings. TCP still exposes a few extras such as the Bluetooth pairing button, HDMI passthrough, temperature, and detailed network diagnostics.

Both modes use HTTP for firmware and network sensors. To switch later, remove and re-add the integration.

grpc_oauth.title: Sign in to Sony
grpc_oauth.description:
Use **Chrome** (recommended) and open developer tools **before** you sign in:

1. Press **F12** → **Network** → enable **Preserve log**
2. Open this sign-in link and complete Sony login for Home Entertainment & Sound Service:

{authorize_url}

3. After login, Chrome tries to open `ssh-app://signin?...` and fails on desktop. That is expected — the URL is **not** shown in the address bar.
4. In **Network**, filter `signin` and find the request to `ssh-app://signin?code=...`. Copy the full URL from **Request URL** or the **Location** header.
5. Select **Submit** below and paste the URL (or just the `code` value) on the next step.

grpc_oauth_callback.title: Paste Sony sign-in redirect
grpc_oauth_callback.description:
Paste the `ssh-app://signin?code=...` URL from Chrome **Network** tools, or paste only the `code` value.

**Chrome:** **F12** → **Network** → filter `signin` → select the `ssh-app://signin` request → copy **Request URL** or the **Location** response header.

grpc_oauth_device.title: Choose Sony device
grpc_oauth_device.description: Your Sony account has more than one device. Select the Bravia Theatre you are setting up.

user_confirm.title: Confirm setup
user_confirm.description: Connection to {host} was successful. Do you want to add this device?

zeroconf_confirm.title: Discovered Bravia Theatre
zeroconf_confirm.description:
Do you want to set up {name}?

Make sure **External control** is enabled in the Sony Home Entertainment Connect app under **Settings > Network settings**.

reauth_confirm.title: Re-authenticate {name}
reauth_confirm.description: The device is no longer reachable. Update the IP address if it has changed. gRPC entries will sign in to Sony again on the next steps.

error.cannot_connect:
Unable to connect to the Bravia Theatre device. Please verify:
- The IP address is correct
- The device is on your network
- For TCP: IP control is enabled and port 33336 is reachable
- For gRPC: port 55051 is reachable and External control is enabled in the Sony app
error.invalid_grpc_keys: Invalid Sony Seeds credentials after sign-in.
error.invalid_oauth_redirect: Could not read an authorization code from your input. In Chrome **Network** tools, copy the full `ssh-app://signin?...` URL or paste only the `code` value.
error.oauth_failed: Sony sign-in failed. Start again and use a fresh login link.

abort.not_bravia_theatre: Discovered device is not a Bravia Theatre speaker
abort.wrong_account: The signed-in Sony account does not match this Bravia Theatre device
abort.not_grpc_transport: gRPC options are only available for gRPC transport entries

init.title: gRPC options
init.description: gRPC transport settings. Seeds cloud reads use the same Sony API as BRAVIA Connect (@mafredri) for settings not readable over local gRPC — required on models where TCP is blocked (e.g. HT-A8).

data.grpc_seeds_poll: Read notify-only settings from Sony Seeds cloud

reload_failed.message: Integration reload failed. Please reload manually via Settings > Devices & Services > Bravia Theatre > Reload

detection_failed.message: Failed to detect subwoofer due to a connection error. Please try again.

firmware_update_failed.message: Failed to trigger firmware update. The device may be busy or unreachable. Please try again later.
